title: Encyclia boothiana
intro: Encyclia boothiana, also known as the Panama orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Central America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia boothiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Encyclia boothiana include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and immune function.
taxonomy: Encyclia boothiana is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Within the family, it is classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Encyclia is further divided into several species, including E. boothiana. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
